summary:
Given two strings, determine whether one is an anagram of the other.

Two strings are anagrams when they contain exactly the same characters with the same frequency, just possibly in a different order. Return true if they match this way and false otherwise.

examples.explanation: Both strings use the letters a, a, a, n, g, r, m, so they are anagrams.
The letter counts differ, so t is not a rearrangement of s.

constraints: s and t consist of lowercase English letters
Strings of different lengths can never be anagrams
